Title: Data Analyst-- AMADC5779657
Description:
Job Summary

The Data Analyst II will collaborate with the GREO team and cross-functional groups across to support reporting, data quality, and analytics initiatives. This role involves managing Anaplan reporting, developing automated data pipelines, delivering management reporting, and supporting compliance and contract-related reporting. The analyst will play a key role in ensuring data accuracy, governance, and operational efficiency across GRE datasets and technology applicatins.




Key Responsibilities

Anaplan Reporting & Data Quality
Support migration of existing reports and control documents into Anaplan
Assist with user testing, validation, and parallel run support
Prepare user guides, training materials, and enablement documentation
Collaborate with the Anaplan Centre of Excellence to test, maintain, and enhance the environment
Develop automated data pipelines between Anaplan and enterprise systems (e.g., data lake, Yardi, VTS, PowerBI)
Implement automated data validation checks to improve data quality
Management Reporting
Deliver monthly and quarterly management reporting and fund-level portfolio analytics




Required Qualifications

Undergraduate degree in a relevant field
5–7 years of experience in data analysis or related roles
Advanced proficiency in Anaplan (Model Builder designation is an asset)
Strong SQL skills, including query optimization
Proficiency with Microsoft Office (advanced Excel preferred)
Proficiency in Python libraries
Proficiency in data modeling (DAX, Power Query/M)
Preferred Qualifications
Familiarity with real estate systems (Yardi, VTS)
Ability to translate complex models into simple terms
Strong knowledge of data modeling and reporting automation




Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and collaboration abilities
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver on deadlines
Detail-oriented with a focus on data accuracy and governance

  9. Familiarity with real estate systems (Yardi, VTS)
  10. Ability to translate complex models into simple terms
  11. Strong knowledge of data modeling and reporting automation


Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  1. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  2. Excellent communication and collaboration abilities
  3. Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver on deadlines
  4. Detail-oriented with a focus on data accuracy and governance

Title: Data Analyst Intern/Co-op (Fall 2026)
Description:
Type De Rôle

Internship/Co-op

Session De Stage

Fall/Term 1

Lieu De Travail

Toronto, Ontario, Canada

Horaire

37.5

Détails De La Rémunération

$45,700 - $74,400 CAD

La TD a à cœur d’offrir une rémunération juste et équitable à tous les collègues. Les occasions de croissance et le perfectionnement des compétences sont des caractéristiques essentielles de l’expérience collègue à la TD. Nos politiques et pratiques en matière de rémunération ont été conçues pour permettre aux collègues de progresser dans l’échelle salariale au fil du temps, à mesure qu’ils s’améliorent dans leurs fonctions. Le salaire de base offert peut varier en fonction des compétences et de l’expérience du candidat, de ses connaissances professionnelles, de son emplacement géographique et d’autres besoins particuliers du secteur et de l’entreprise.

En tant que candidat, nous vous encourageons à poser des questions sur la rémunération et à avoir une conversation franche avec votre recruteur, qui pourra vous fournir des détails plus précis sur ce poste.

Department Overview

Description du poste :

Co-op and Internship opportunities allow you to gain valuable work experience across a number of the businesses at TD. You will work with experienced colleagues, receive world class training, and be part of a community of students across TD, where you will have an impact, grow as individual and experience our culture of care.

Role

Our Co-op/Intern Programming is offered with select Co-op and Internship roles, and is designed to help you better understand the TD business, build on critical career capabilities, and broaden your professional network. This program is designed to complement your on-the job experience and features:


Leadership talks with key Leaders from across the organization
Diversity and Inclusion and Personal Branding and so much more


Job Description

You will have the opportunity to work with one of our various teams within the (Data-as-a-service or DaaS). From Day 1, you will be treated as an equal member of the team and given meaningful assignments to help us deliver improvements to our data platforms across multiple segments such as Core Banking, Business Banking, Insurance, Wealth, or Enterprise Platforms.

Responsibilities

Job responsibilities for a junior Data Analyst may vary by area. This may include, but is not limited to the following:


Work with business and technology partners to define, develop and document detailed business, data and system requirements.
Participate as a member of agile pod team focused on developing new capabilities, including agile ceremonies.
Collaborate with data engineers, product owners, quality engineers and other stakeholders to deliver an end-to-end capability from concept to production-deployment.
Have performed data analysis, profiling, clean-up, data wrangling and validation as part of University assignments.
Based on data analysis, provide recommendations for data remediation, improvements.
Participate in program/ system research, analysis, detailed design and problem resolution.
Respond to user enquiries on data platform, in a timely manner.
Troubleshoot and determine root cause and resolution for application defects.
Apply Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Project Management Methodology (PMLC) to complete specific tasks for projects. 
Continuously enhance knowledge/expertise and keep up-to-date with leading-edge technologies trends/developments.
Develop expertise in data platform, infrastructure, data pipeline, analytical tools and design techniques that can contribute to effective solution development and delivery.
Good communication, interpersonal, collaboration skills to work in an agile cross-functional team, with different stakeholders.
Have owned, delivered a project assignment in University starting from Inception, Initiation, Execution until Implementation. Project outcome reviewed and measured as expected.


Job Requirements


Currently enrolled in a relevant technology programs, such as Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology Management, Computing, etc.
Strong communication and inter-personal skills with the ability to deliver message and ideas effectively to diverse audiences such as business, development, quality engineers and management stakeholders.
A thirst for constantly exploring emerging technology such as Azure, python, Talend, Tableau, Informatica.
Familiarity with Jira and Confluence.
Proficient with Excel, Visio, SQL, some reporting and analytical tools.
Understands Data Modeling concepts.
Able to manage multiple priorities and work independently, as well as part of a team.
Experience with technology project delivery and agile delivery methodology would be an asset.
Some prior experience with creating visualization such as Process and Data Flows.
Good documentation skill; can produce data mapping between Source and Target Systems. Capture User Stories and Use Cases.
Hands-on programming experience would be an asset, which could include familiarity and strength in any of the following: C, C++, C#, Object C, Java, Java Script, Python, NodeJS, SQL, J2EE, Web Services, DB2, MS SQL.


Additional Information

Please note that this is a general posting. If you are selected for an interview, more information regarding which business group and the specific job duties will be provided. Additionally, we are open to all years of study but primarily target 3rd & 4th year students.

Early Talent Requirements


This position is a 4-month work term and will commence September 8th – December 18th, 2026.
Applications must include a resume (maximum of 2 pages) and a school transcript
Must be enrolled in an undergraduate/ graduate degree (requirement of all co-op/intern positions) 
We welcome all applications; however, we will only contact qualified candidates chosen for an interview. Thank you for your interest.
Selected candidates will be invited to participate in a technical assessment
TD requires employees to reside in Canada where the role is located. Successful candidates are expected to arrange relocation to one of our Toronto offices in a hybrid model (4 days per week in office).


Assigned Line Of Business Requirements

Please note that the line of business that you are assigned to may require specific skills not mentioned in this posting

HOURS

Monday-Friday, standard business hours

OFFICE LOCATIONS

We have offices located in the Greater Toronto Area, including Markham, Mississauga, Scarborough and Toronto as well as offices in London, ON. Please note that your specific office location will be determined based on business needs.

INCLUSIVENESS

At TD, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected and supported. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our customers and communities in which we live and serve. If you require an accommodation for the recruitment/interview process (including alternate formats of materials, or accessible meeting rooms or other accommodation), please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

À propos de nous

La TD est un chef de file mondial dans le secteur des institutions financières. Elle est la cinquième banque en importance en Amérique du Nord de par son nombre de succursales. Chaque jour, nous nous efforçons de rendre chaque interaction, chaque produit et chaque expérience remarquablement humaine et agréablement simple à plus de 27 millions de ménages et d’entreprises au Canada, aux États-Unis et partout dans le monde. Plus de 95 000 collègues de la TD mettent à profit leurs compétences, leur talent et leur créativité pour approfondir les relations, assurer une exécution rigoureuse et rendre l’expérience bancaire plus simple et plus rapide. La TD est une entreprise profondément engagée à être une leader en matière d’expérience client. Voilà pourquoi nous croyons que chaque collègue, peu importe son secteur d’activité, est en contact avec la clientèle. Ensemble, nous réimaginons l’expérience bancaire et ses possibilités pour notre clientèle, nos collègues et nos collectivités.

Notre programme de rémunération globale

Notre programme de rémunération globale reflète les investissements que nous faisons pour aider nos collègues et leur famille à atteindre leurs objectifs en matière de bien-être mental, physique et financier. La rémunération globale à la TD inclut le salaire de base, la rémunération variable et bien d’autres régimes clés, comme des avantages sociaux en matière de santé et de bien-être, des régimes d’épargne et de retraite, des congés payés, des avantages bancaires et des rabais, des occasions de développement de carrière et des programmes de récompenses et reconnaissance. En savoir plus

Renseignements Supplémentaires

Nous sommes ravis que vous envisagiez une carrière à la TD. Sachez que nous avons à cœur d’aider nos collègues à réussir dans leur vie tant personnelle que professionnelle. C’est d’ailleurs pourquoi nous leur offrons des conversations sur le perfectionnement, des programmes de formation et un régime d’avantages sociaux concurrentiel.

Perfectionnement des collègues

Un cheminement professionnel précis vous intéresse ou bien vous cherchez à perfectionner certaines compétences? Nous tenons à vous mettre sur la voie de la réussite. Vous aurez des conversations régulières sur le développement de carrière, le perfectionnement et le rendement avec votre gestionnaire. Une variété de programmes de mentorat et une plateforme d’apprentissage en ligne seront également à votre disposition pour vous aider à ouvrir de nouvelles portes.

Si vous avez à cœur d’aider vos clients et de nouer des relations solides et durables, la TD offre divers parcours de carrière où vous pouvez accroître votre expertise et avoir une incidence significative.

Nous sommes déterminés à appuyer votre réussite et à favoriser un milieu de travail respectueux où la diversité des points de vue est valorisée, où chacun a les mêmes occasions de progresser et où vous pouvez vous réaliser pleinement et atteindre vos objectifs de carrière. À la TD, nous attirons et perfectionnons les meilleurs talents.

Formation et intégration

Nous tenons à nous assurer que vous disposez des outils et ressources nécessaires pour réussir à votre nouveau poste. Dans cette optique, nous organiserons des séances d’intégration et de formation.

Processus d’entrevue

Nous communiquerons avec les candidats sélectionnés pour planifier une entrevue. Nous ferons notre possible pour communiquer par courriel ou par téléphone avec tous les candidats pour leur faire part de notre décision.

Mesures d’adaptation

L’accessibilité est importante pour nous. N’hésitez pas à nous faire part de toute mesure d’adaptation (salles de réunion accessibles, sous-titres pour les entrevues virtuelles, etc.) dont vous pourriez avoir besoin pour participer sans entraves au processus d’entrevue.

Nous avons hâte d’avoir de vos nouvelles!

Title: Analyste de données / Data Analyst
Description:
Analyste de données




À propos de MTY

MTY Group est un chef de file mondial du franchisage multi-marques (90+ marques, 7 000 établissements). Nous renforçons une culture axée sur les données où la qualité et l’intégrité des informations soutiennent des décisions éclairées dans chaque fonction.




Aperçu du rôle

Êtes-vous un passionné de données motivé par la transformation de données complexes en informations exploitables ? Recherchez-vous un rôle stimulant et dynamique qui vous permet de mettre à profit vos compétences analytiques afin d’avoir un impact concret ? Souhaitez-vous faire évoluer et approfondir vos connaissances ? Si oui, l’équipe Data et Analytics a une opportunité passionnante à vous offrir !




Nous recherchons un Analyste de données combinant rigueur analytique, compréhension d’affaires/processus, et exigence élevée en qualité/intégrité des données. Vous collaborerez avec les équipes (Ventes, Opérations, Marketing, Finances, Chaîne d’approvisionnement, RH) pour cartographier les processus, définir les indicateurs et transformer nos produits/actifs de données en valeur d’affaires mesurable.




Le poste s’appuie sur une plateforme moderne dans le cloud (Azure + Databricks) et sur des outils tel que copilotes BI/SQL, Databricks Assistant et services Azure OpenAI pour accélérer l’analyse. SQL est obligatoire; Python est un atout.




Avantages

3 semaines de vacances;
5 journées de congés maladies;
2000$ de remboursement des frais d’ordre professionnel et de formation continue;
Horairede travail flexible
Demi-journée le vendredi disponible à l’année;
Plans de santé et dentaires d'entreprise, compte de dépenses santé/personnel de bien-être supplémentaire de 300$;
Programme d'assistance aux employés avec accès à un large éventail de services allant de la santé mentale aux conseils juridiques et financiers;
Régime de participation des employés aux bénéfices avec contribution de l'employeur;
Prix mensuels de reconnaissance à l'échelle de l'entreprise avec des gagnants trimestriels et annuels;
Événements sociaux d'entreprise, y compris, mais sans s'y limiter, des webinaires, des assemblées publiques trimestrielles et des activités amusantes pour tous;
Tenue d'affaires décontractée;
Stationnement gratuit au bureau.




Responsabilités

Affaires & processus

Co-définir les objectifs, processus et besoins décisionnels; les traduire en exigences analytiques et KPIs.
Cartographier les processus actuels/cibles et identifier des pistes d’automatisation/standardisation via des produits de données.

Analyse & création de valeur

Analyser de grands volumes de données multi-sources; produire des recommandations claires.
Concevoir et fiabiliser des produits de données réutilisables (modèles sémantiques, couches de métriques, jeux de données certifiés) alignés sur les processus et contrats de données.
Créer des tableaux de bord (Power BI/Excel) et accompagner les utilisateurs dans l’interprétation.

Qualité, intégrité & gouvernance

Définir des règles de qualité (exactitude, exhaustivité, fraîcheur), suivre des SLA, traiter les écarts, documenter la lignée et les définitions.
Travailler dans les schémas d’accès/gouvernance Unity Catalog/Purview; promouvoir des définitions de métriques cohérentes et des contrats de données.

Cloud & IA

Exploiter le Lakehouse Databricks (tables Delta, SQL, notebooks) et les services Azure pour consommer des données gouvernées.
Utiliser des assistants IA (Databricks Assistant, copilotes BI/SQL) pour accélérer les requêtes, résumer et prototyper l’analyse de façon responsable.




Profil recherché

Expérience : 2+ ans en analytique/intelligence d’affaires avec impacts démontrables.
Indispensable : SQL avancé (requêtes complexes, CTE, fenêtres, performance).
Atout : Python (pandas/notebooks) pour analyses et automatisation légères.
Outils : Power BI (DAX, modélisation, mesures, RLS), Excel; exposition à Databricks et Azure (ADLS, Databricks SQL/Unity Catalog, Purview) ou plateformes cloud équivalentes.
Sens des affaires : Cartographie des processus, définition de KPIs, capacité à quantifier les résultats.
Qualité des données : Validation, réconciliation et documentation systématiques; résultats fiables et explicables.
Communication : Vulgarisation claire, animation des parties prenantes, accompagnement des utilisateurs.




Atouts supplémentaires

Expérience sur des couches de métriques et modèles sémantiques.
Pratique de l’IA générative pour l’analytique (patrons de prompts, POC copilotes) avec garde-fous responsables.
Notions d’expérimentation/tests A/B et d’analyse causale.




Veuillez noter que toute offre d'emploi sera conditionnelle à une vérification des antécédents, y compris du casier judiciaire.

*La majorité de notre clientèle et une grande partie de nos employés sont en dehors du Québec*




---




Data Analyst




Role Overview

Are you a data enthusiast motivated to turn complex data into actionable insights? Are you looking for a challenging and dynamic role that allows you to use your analytical skills to make a real impact? Are you looking to grow your knowledge? If so, MTY Data and Analytics team have an exciting opportunity for you!




We’re seeking a Data Analyst who blends sharp analytics with deep business/process understanding and a relentless commitment to data quality and integrity. You’ll partner with leaders across Sales, Operations, Marketing, Finance, Supply Chain, and HR to map processes, define KPIs, and turn data products and assets into measurable business value.

This role operates on our modern cloud stack (Azure + Databricks) and embraces practical AI tooling (e.g., BI/SQL copilots, Databricks Assistant, Azure OpenAI-powered capabilities) to accelerate insight generation. SQL is required; Python is a plus.




Benefits

3 weeks of vacation;
5 days of sick leave;
$2000 reimbursement for professional order costs and continuing education requirements;
Flexible working hours;
Half-day available every Friday year-round;
Company health and dental plans, with an additional $300 personal wellness expense account;
Employee assistance program with access to a wide range of services from mental health to legal and financial counseling;
Employee profit-sharing plan with employer contribution;
Monthly company-wide recognition awards with quarterly and annual winners;
Company social events, including but not limited to webinars, quarterly town halls, and fun activities for all;
Casual dress code;
Free parking at the office.




What you’ll do

Business & Process

Partner with domain stakeholders to understand goals, processes, and decision workflows; translate them into clear analytical requirements and KPIs.
Map “as-is” vs. “to-be” processes and identify automation/standardization opportunities using data products.

Analysis & Value Realization

Explore and analyze large, multi-source datasets; develop clear narratives and decision options.
Build, iterate, and govern reusable data products (subject-area semantic models, metrics layers, certified datasets) that align to business processes and data contracts.
Deliver self-service dashboards and reports (Power BI/Excel) and coach users on interpreting insights.

Data Quality, Integrity & Governance

Define data quality rules (accuracy, completeness, timeliness), monitor with SLAs, reconcile exceptions, and document lineage/definitions.
Work within Unity Catalog/Purview access and governance patterns; champion consistent metric definitions and data contracts.

Cloud & AI Enablement

Leverage Databricks Lakehouse (Delta tables, SQL, notebooks) and Azure data services to consume curated, governed data.
Use AI assistants (e.g., Databricks Assistant, BI/SQL copilots) to speed up querying, summarize findings, and prototype analyses responsibly.
Collaborate with Data Engineering to influence modeling (dimensional/semantic).




Qualifications

Experience: 2+ years in data analysis/business intelligence or adjacent roles with demonstrable business impact.
Must-have: Strong SQL for complex querying, joins, CTEs, window functions, performance-aware analysis.
Nice-to-have: Python for analysis/automation (pandas, notebooks) and light data wrangling.
Tools: Power BI (DAX, data modeling, measures, row-level security), Excel; exposure to Databricks and Azure (ADLS, Databricks SQL/Unity Catalog, Purview) or comparable cloud analytics platforms.
Business acumen: Process mapping, KPI definition, ability to frame trade-offs and quantify outcomes.
Quality mindset: Habitual validation, reconciliation, and documentation; care for trustworthy, explainable results.
Communication: Crisp storytelling, stakeholder facilitation, and enablement of non-technical audiences.




Nice to have

Experience defining/operationalizing metrics layers and semantic models; familiarity with data contracts.

HTML:
Analyste de données


À propos de MTY

MTY Group est un chef de file mondial du franchisage multi-marques (90+ marques, 7 000 établissements). Nous renforçons une culture axée sur les données où la qualité et l’intégrité des informations soutiennent des décisions éclairées dans chaque fonction.


Aperçu du rôle

Êtes-vous un passionné de données motivé par la transformation de données complexes en informations exploitables ? Recherchez-vous un rôle stimulant et dynamique qui vous permet de mettre à profit vos compétences analytiques afin d’avoir un impact concret ? Souhaitez-vous faire évoluer et approfondir vos connaissances ? Si oui, l’équipe Data et Analytics a une opportunité passionnante à vous offrir !


Nous recherchons un Analyste de données combinant rigueur analytique, compréhension d’affaires/processus, et exigence élevée en qualité/intégrité des données. Vous collaborerez avec les équipes (Ventes, Opérations, Marketing, Finances, Chaîne d’approvisionnement, RH) pour cartographier les processus, définir les indicateurs et transformer nos produits/actifs de données en valeur d’affaires mesurable.


Le poste s’appuie sur une plateforme moderne dans le cloud (Azure + Databricks) et sur des outils tel que copilotes BI/SQL, Databricks Assistant et services Azure OpenAI pour accélérer l’analyse. SQL est obligatoire; Python est un atout.


Avantages

  • 3 semaines de vacances;
  • 5 journées de congés maladies;
  • 2000$ de remboursement des frais d’ordre professionnel et de formation continue;
  • Horairede travail flexible
  • Demi-journée le vendredi disponible à l’année;
  • Plans de santé et dentaires d'entreprise, compte de dépenses santé/personnel de bien-être supplémentaire de 300$;
  • Programme d'assistance aux employés avec accès à un large éventail de services allant de la santé mentale aux conseils juridiques et financiers;
  • Régime de participation des employés aux bénéfices avec contribution de l'employeur;
  • Prix mensuels de reconnaissance à l'échelle de l'entreprise avec des gagnants trimestriels et annuels;
  • Événements sociaux d'entreprise, y compris, mais sans s'y limiter, des webinaires, des assemblées publiques trimestrielles et des activités amusantes pour tous;
  • Tenue d'affaires décontractée;
  • Stationnement gratuit au bureau.


Responsabilités

Affaires & processus

  • Co-définir les objectifs, processus et besoins décisionnels; les traduire en exigences analytiques et KPIs.
  • Cartographier les processus actuels/cibles et identifier des pistes d’automatisation/standardisation via des produits de données.

Analyse & création de valeur

  • Analyser de grands volumes de données multi-sources; produire des recommandations claires.
  • Concevoir et fiabiliser des produits de données réutilisables (modèles sémantiques, couches de métriques, jeux de données certifiés) alignés sur les processus et contrats de données.
  • Créer des tableaux de bord (Power BI/Excel) et accompagner les utilisateurs dans l’interprétation.

Qualité, intégrité & gouvernance

  • Définir des règles de qualité (exactitude, exhaustivité, fraîcheur), suivre des SLA, traiter les écarts, documenter la lignée et les définitions.
  • Travailler dans les schémas d’accès/gouvernance Unity Catalog/Purview; promouvoir des définitions de métriques cohérentes et des contrats de données.

Cloud & IA

  • Exploiter le Lakehouse Databricks (tables Delta, SQL, notebooks) et les services Azure pour consommer des données gouvernées.
  • Utiliser des assistants IA (Databricks Assistant, copilotes BI/SQL) pour accélérer les requêtes, résumer et prototyper l’analyse de façon responsable.


Profil recherché

  • Expérience : 2+ ans en analytique/intelligence d’affaires avec impacts démontrables.
  • Indispensable : SQL avancé (requêtes complexes, CTE, fenêtres, performance).
  • Atout : Python (pandas/notebooks) pour analyses et automatisation légères.
  • Outils : Power BI (DAX, modélisation, mesures, RLS), Excel; exposition à Databricks et Azure (ADLS, Databricks SQL/Unity Catalog, Purview) ou plateformes cloud équivalentes.
  • Sens des affaires : Cartographie des processus, définition de KPIs, capacité à quantifier les résultats.
  • Qualité des données : Validation, réconciliation et documentation systématiques; résultats fiables et explicables.
  • Communication : Vulgarisation claire, animation des parties prenantes, accompagnement des utilisateurs.


Atouts supplémentaires

  • Expérience sur des couches de métriques et modèles sémantiques.
  • Pratique de l’IA générative pour l’analytique (patrons de prompts, POC copilotes) avec garde-fous responsables.
  • Notions d’expérimentation/tests A/B et d’analyse causale.


Veuillez noter que toute offre d'emploi sera conditionnelle à une vérification des antécédents, y compris du casier judiciaire.

*La majorité de notre clientèle et une grande partie de nos employés sont en dehors du Québec*


---


Data Analyst


Role Overview

Are you a data enthusiast motivated to turn complex data into actionable insights? Are you looking for a challenging and dynamic role that allows you to use your analytical skills to make a real impact? Are you looking to grow your knowledge? If so, MTY Data and Analytics team have an exciting opportunity for you!


We’re seeking a Data Analyst who blends sharp analytics with deep business/process understanding and a relentless commitment to data quality and integrity. You’ll partner with leaders across Sales, Operations, Marketing, Finance, Supply Chain, and HR to map processes, define KPIs, and turn data products and assets into measurable business value.

This role operates on our modern cloud stack (Azure + Databricks) and embraces practical AI tooling (e.g., BI/SQL copilots, Databricks Assistant, Azure OpenAI-powered capabilities) to accelerate insight generation. SQL is required; Python is a plus.


Benefits

  • 3 weeks of vacation;
  • 5 days of sick leave;
  • $2000 reimbursement for professional order costs and continuing education requirements;
  • Flexible working hours;
  • Half-day available every Friday year-round;
  • Company health and dental plans, with an additional $300 personal wellness expense account;
  • Employee assistance program with access to a wide range of services from mental health to legal and financial counseling;
  • Employee profit-sharing plan with employer contribution;
  • Monthly company-wide recognition awards with quarterly and annual winners;
  • Company social events, including but not limited to webinars, quarterly town halls, and fun activities for all;
  • Casual dress code;
  • Free parking at the office.


What you’ll do

Business & Process

  • Partner with domain stakeholders to understand goals, processes, and decision workflows; translate them into clear analytical requirements and KPIs.
  • Map “as-is” vs. “to-be” processes and identify automation/standardization opportunities using data products.

Analysis & Value Realization

  • Explore and analyze large, multi-source datasets; develop clear narratives and decision options.
  • Build, iterate, and govern reusable data products (subject-area semantic models, metrics layers, certified datasets) that align to business processes and data contracts.
  • Deliver self-service dashboards and reports (Power BI/Excel) and coach users on interpreting insights.

Data Quality, Integrity & Governance

  • Define data quality rules (accuracy, completeness, timeliness), monitor with SLAs, reconcile exceptions, and document lineage/definitions.
  • Work within Unity Catalog/Purview access and governance patterns; champion consistent metric definitions and data contracts.

Cloud & AI Enablement

  • Leverage Databricks Lakehouse (Delta tables, SQL, notebooks) and Azure data services to consume curated, governed data.
  • Use AI assistants (e.g., Databricks Assistant, BI/SQL copilots) to speed up querying, summarize findings, and prototype analyses responsibly.
  • Collaborate with Data Engineering to influence modeling (dimensional/semantic).


Qualifications

  • Experience: 2+ years in data analysis/business intelligence or adjacent roles with demonstrable business impact.
  • Must-have: Strong SQL for complex querying, joins, CTEs, window functions, performance-aware analysis.
  • Nice-to-have: Python for analysis/automation (pandas, notebooks) and light data wrangling.
  • Tools: Power BI (DAX, data modeling, measures, row-level security), Excel; exposure to Databricks and Azure (ADLS, Databricks SQL/Unity Catalog, Purview) or comparable cloud analytics platforms.
  • Business acumen: Process mapping, KPI definition, ability to frame trade-offs and quantify outcomes.
  • Quality mindset: Habitual validation, reconciliation, and documentation; care for trustworthy, explainable results.
  • Communication: Crisp storytelling, stakeholder facilitation, and enablement of non-technical audiences.


Nice to have

  • Experience defining/operationalizing metrics layers and semantic models; familiarity with data contracts.
  • Hands-on with GenAI for analytics (prompt patterns, proof-of-concept copilots) with responsible-AI guardrails.
  • Familiarity with experimentation/AB testing and causal analysis in operational settings.


Please note that any offer of employment will be conditional upon a background check, including a criminal record check.

*The majority of our clients and a large proportion of our employees are outside Quebec*

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Enterprise Analytics Data Analyst – 6 month contract with possible extension




Our client is a global leader in professional services. In this role you will work with business teams delivery Enterprise Data Analytics solutions that help the organization make strategic decisions.




The Role

Work with business teams to explore their current technical and data landscape and to complete discovery phase activities, in preparation for requirements elicitation.
Define and document business requirements, technical requirements, and functional specifications for new projects and change requests, ensuring all stakeholders are consulted and informed.
Manage stakeholder expectations and ensure all documented requirements are fully reviewed and approved.
Collaborate with data engineers and architects across Azure-based data platforms (ADF, Databricks, Synapse) to support end-to-end data solutions
Support the Enterprise Analytics team in troubleshooting operational issues.
Map and analyze data flows across modern data platforms (e.g., ADF, Databricks, Synapse), ensuring traceability, lineage, and data integrity from source to consumption
Complete analysis on datasets of varying sizes and clearly communicate the resulting insights to stakeholders, enabling them to make informed business decisions.
Support analysis and validation of data pipelines, including ingestion, transformation, and data movement across systems
Support the definition and execution of data quality checks, including reconciliation, anomaly detection, and validation of transformation logic across pipelines
Collaborate with Quality Assurance analysts during their testing of Enterprise Analytics projects.
Engage with the Enterprise Analytics team to continuously improve the program’s methodology, including document templates, pipeline management and reporting solutions.
Work with the Data Management team to support and maintain data analytics standards across the organization, ensuring relevant data standards and policies are observed in all Enterprise Analytics projects.
Support initiatives involving Microsoft Fabric, including understanding of Lakehouse architecture and semantic data models for reporting and analytics




Requirements

Minimum 5 years of experience in a data analysis role, including large-scale data transformation projects.
Ability to analyze and document data source systems, data objects, linage, applications, data usage and privacy.
Exceptional communication skills, including the ability to deliver concise requirements and flow diagrams.
Ability to forge consensus within a group of stakeholders, to deliver a solution that works for all.
Knowledge of agile methodology and the software development life cycle (SDLC).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, a creative approach, and superior design aesthetics.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases (e.g., SQL Server, Oracle), with exposure to modern cloud-based data platforms
Experience with modern Azure-based data platforms, including Azure Data Factory, Databricks (Spark), Synapse Analytics, and exposure to Microsoft Fabric; with a solid understanding of data pipelines, Lakehouse architecture, and large-scale data processing.
Understanding of data governance concepts, including data lineage, data classification, and handling of sensitive/PII data

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Description:

Data Analyst Intern

Cloud Data Platforms (AWS, Azure, Snowflake, Databricks)

About The Internship

We are seeking a Data Analyst Intern to support our data and analytics initiatives across AWS and Azure cloud platforms. This role is ideal for a student eager to gain hands‑on experience with data lakes, data warehouses, and scalable analytics platforms, including Snowflake and/or Databricks.

You will work alongside experienced Software R&D team members on analyzing customer data and implementing ML algorithms to transform system and application data into actionable business insights, with opportunities to support machine learning model development and deployment.

Compensation & Role Details:

Expected Salary Range: The base hourly rate for this role is expected to be between $20–$23 CAD, depending on experience and qualifications.

Role Type: New Role

AI Disclosure: AI may be used during the hiring process.

What You’ll Do


Assist with ingesting, transforming, and validating data in cloud-based data lakes and warehouses
Support ETL / ELT pipelines using SQL, Python, R, and/or Scala
Work with Snowflake to build analytical tables and views
Use Databricks (Spark / PySpark / SQL) for data processing and exploratory analysis
Analyze datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business insights
Assist with data preparation, feature engineering, and evaluation for ML use cases
Support basic ML workflows such as batch inference, experiments, or model monitoring
Monitor data pipelines, investigate issues, and document findings
Follow best practices for data security, governance, and cloud access controls


Required Qualifications


Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s program in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, AI, or a related field
Strong foundational knowledge of SQL
Experience with Python for data analysis or scripting
Understanding of core data concepts (tables, joins, aggregations, schemas)
Introductory knowledge of machine learning concepts
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Clear communication skills and ability to document work


Nice to Have


Exposure to AWS (S3, Glue, Athena) or Azure (Data Factory, Synapse, ADLS)
Familiarity with Snowflake, Databricks, or Apache Spark
Experience with notebooks (Jupyter), Git, or cloud-based data projects
Interest in data engineering, analytics engineering, or ML engineering careers


What You’ll Gain


Hands‑on experience with production cloud data platforms
Exposure to Snowflake and Databricks in real-world use cases
Mentorship from experienced data and machine learning engineers
Practical understanding of the full data lifecycle—from ingestion to insights to ML
A strong foundation for a career in Data Engineering or Machine Learning Engineering

Leonardo

Scheduled Weekly Hours:

37.5

Number of Openings Available:

1

Student (Fixed Term) (Trainee)

More About Jonas Software:

Jonas Software is a leading provider of enterprise management software solutions, serving a wide range of vertical markets including hospitality, healthcare, construction, education, personal care, fitness, leisure, moving and legal services, to name a few. Within these markets, Jonas is comprised of over 65 distinct brands, each a respected leader in its domain.

Jonas’ vision is to be the branded global leader across these verticals and to be recognized by customers and industry stakeholders as the trusted provider of “Software for Life.” We are committed to technology, product innovation, quality, and exceptional customer service.

Jonas Software supports over 60,000 customers in more than 30 countries. We employ over 6,000 skilled professionals, including industry experts and technology specialists. Across our broader network, we support a global workforce of more than 30,000 employees.

Headquartered in Canada, Jonas Software has a global footprint with offices around the world. We’re a 100% owned subsidiary of Constellation Software Inc., based in Toronto, publicly listed on the TSX (CSU.TO), and a member of the S&P/TSX 60 Index.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Optimize Wealth Management:

Optimize Wealth Management is a Canadian portfolio management firm dedicated to building wealth with intention. We leverage cutting-edge in-house technology to deliver a seamless client experience, offering a full suite of family office services. Our platform empowers financial professionals to provide exceptional, tech-enabled service to their clients.




The opportunity:

We are seeking a meticulous and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our back-office team at our downtown Toronto office. In this role, you will play a key part in managing and improving the integrity, structure, and efficiency of the firm’s data systems. You will work closely with internal teams to maintain and enhance our CRM data infrastructure, build scalable solutions to support analysis, and ensure data accuracy across multiple sources. This is a full-time, on-site role (Monday to Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.).




Key responsibilities:

Develop, implement, and maintain data systems and workflows within the firm’s CRM platform.
Evaluate internal systems and processes for efficiency and recommend improvements.
Acquire, clean, and integrate data from multiple internal and external sources.
Create and maintain scripts, queries, and reporting tools to support scalable data analysis.
Ensure data consistency, accuracy, and reliability across operational systems.
Collaborate with team members to support reporting and analytics needs related to client portfolios and investment accounts.




Qualifications:

Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in data management or analysis.
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
Experience working with CRM systems such as HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar platforms.
Proficiency in database management and familiarity with SQL, Python, or advanced Excel formulas.
Ability to prioritize tasks and work accurately under pressure in a fast-paced environment.

Title: Analyst, Data Governance
Description:
What You Will Do


Data Governance Leadership: Support the development, implementation, maintenance, monitoring, and enforcement of data governance standards, policies, procedures, and controls to ensure adherence to privacy, compliance, risk, regulatory, and legal requirements. This includes AI Governance to ensure ethical use and compliance with regulatory standards for AI-driven processes.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Collaborate with leadership, IT departments, business units, Legal, Compliance, Privacy, Risk teams, data producers and consumers, and IT data platform teams to align on data governance goals and support the operationalization of a robust data governance framework.
Promote data literacy by developing materials and conducting training on best practices.
Policy Development: Assist in the creation and enforcement of data management policies to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
Contribute to shaping the data governance accountabilities and operating model and enable data stewardship.
Data Quality Assurance: Contribute to the enhancement and implementation of robust data quality frameworks and conduct regular audits to maintain high data standards.
Support the development and evolution of data governance metrics and KPIs; monitor & measure the overall efficacy of the data governance program.
Proactively monitor data, metadata, and reference data to ensure quality across the CTB data lifecycle.
Implement and leverage data governance tools and platforms to enable capabilities; manage and maintain metadata and other data governance artifacts such as glossaries, classifications, and lineage.
Risk Management: Identify potential risks related to data governance and develop mitigation strategies.
Support data sharing and collaboration across teams and departments; ensure proper data access, usability, and monitoring of data sharing agreements.
Project Oversight: Contribute to large-scale data governance projects from inception to completion.
Stay updated on industry trends, standards, and regulatory requirements related to data governance; recommend appropriate modifications to existing policies and practices.


What You Bring


Post-secondary education in Computer Science, Information Systems Management, or a related field.
Proven experience with corporate data environments supporting cross-functional governance.
Proficient in PL\SQL and confident working in a structured data environment.
Knowledge of business intelligence, modern data warehousing techniques, cloud solutions such as Azure or GCP.
Experience with ETL/ELT processes and modern integration frameworks like API and event streaming.
Experience in IBM Cloud Pak for Data and/or Databricks Unity Catalog.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, and Excel.
Expertise in creating/enforcing data governance policies to ensure data integrity, privacy, compliance, security.
Ability to work with Legal, Privacy, Compliance, and Risk partners
Hands-on experience with data analysis, quality assessment, metadata management.
Excellent communication skills for presenting complex concepts to non-technical audiences.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail; ability to problem-solve and handle ambiguity.
Effective stakeholder management; ability to build trusted relationships and influence decision-making.
Experience in process design/improvement initiatives and leveraging automation.
CDMP certification is an asset.


We’re always looking for great talent! In addition to competitive pay, we offer:


Comprehensive benefits and retirement programs
Performance incentives, Continuing Education Programs
Other perks to support your well-being
Career growth opportunities and product discounts


Our typical hiring range is between $53,000 and $88,000 Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, job-related knowledge, skills and competencies, market location, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements.

About Us

At Canadian Tire Services Limited/Canadian Tire Bank, it is our mandate to continue to create innovative and rewarding financial solutions for our customers. Our growing suite of products and services showcase the dynamic contributions from our employees and our success is driven by a strong vision, loyal customers, and our ability to build teams that reflect the diverse customers and communities in which we live and work. Join us, where there's a place for you here.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

We are committed to fostering an environment where belonging thrives, and diversity, inclusion and equity are infused into everything we do. We believe in building an organizational culture where people are consistently treated with dignity while respecting individual religion, nationality, gender, race, age, perceived ability, spoken language, sexual orientation, and identification. We are united in our purpose of being here to help make life in Canada better. .

Accommodations

We stand firm in our Core Value that inclusion is a must. We welcome and encourage candidates from equity-seeking groups such as people who identify as racialized, Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, women, people with disabilities, and beyond. Should you require any accommodation in applying for this role, or throughout the interview process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to help meet your needs.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Job title: Business Data analys

Location: Brampton, ON (Hybrid)

Employment Type: Contract 18+ Month

Reporting To: Manager, Tax Data & Audit Analytics

Salary: $25/hr - $30/hr on T4




A hands-on technical test (SQL, Python, Excel, Power Query).




Role Summary

The Senior Financial Analyst (SFA) is a critical technical contributor within the Tax Data & Audit Analytics team. This role serves as the data and process backbone of the organization’s tax audit response, delivering accurate, defensible, and audit-ready data to expert tax teams and auditors.
The SFA is responsible for executing detailed data analytics, reconciliations, and reporting across multiple tax streams. This is a hands-on role requiring advanced technical skills in SQL, Excel, ERP data, and dashboarding tools. The successful candidate will investigate data anomalies at a granular level, automate repeatable processes, and ensure the integrity and traceability of all audit evidence.




Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field.
Advanced proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.
Strong expertise in Microsoft Excel, including Power Query and advanced formulas.
Hands-on experience working with large datasets from ERP systems (e.g., SAP or similar).
Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and documentation skills with strong attention to detail.

Title: Sr Data Analyst
Description:
Sr Data Analyst

Client: Public

Location: Edmonton, AL

6 Months Contract

The Data Analyst will primarily work remotely; however, the Data Analyst may be required to attend meetings or work sessions in Edmonton on reasonable notice




Data Engineering:

- Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines to ingest, transform, and store large volumes of data both on-premises (SSIS etc.) and in the cloud (Azure, AWS, GCP)

- Ensure data integrity, accuracy, and availability for analytical and operational use.

- Collaborate with software engineers, data scientists, and business stakeholders to understand data requirements and implement solutions.

- Optimize and improve existing data workflows and ETL processes.

- Implement data governance and security best practices.

- Manage data storage solutions, such as data lakes and data warehouses.

- A successful history of manipulating, processing and extracting value from large, disconnected datasets.

- Deploy data solutions in the cloud and with database tools (SQL, NoSQL).




Data Analytics:

- Perform exploratory data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights.

- Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and visualizations using BI tools (e.g., Power BI, SaaS VA).

- Conduct statistical analysis and modeling to support business strategies.

- Communicate findings and recommendations to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner.

- Use SQL and other query languages to extract and manipulate data from various sources.

- Create plans and strategies to integrate data models and improve service delivery.

- Develop and share service metrics and performance dashboards.

- Facilitate the frequent delivery of services and deliverables to ensure the successful creation of the target state while mentoring Agile Teams to grow their analytics fluency.

- Support corporate priorities with data-driven evidence using analytics, visualizations, and data modeling.




Note: AI-enabled tools may be used to sort applications based on job-related criteria. All AI generated results are vetted by our team and the decision of which candidates move forward is always made by a human.

Title: Azure Data Analyst
Description:
Position Description

This role is hybrid and requires you to be at our downtown Toronto and/or Client office at a minimum 2 days per week - subject to change at any time.

The Azure Data Analyst will play a critical role in designing, analyzing, and delivering enterprise‑scale data solutions within an Azure cloud ecosystem. This role requires a strong combination of data design expertise, business analysis skills, and domain knowledge in personal banking products. The candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and agile delivery teams to translate business needs into high‑quality, scalable data models and solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Data Design & Architecture


Design and maintain logical and physical data models aligned with business requirements and enterprise standards.
Define end to end data mappings from source systems to target data platforms, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Perform data profiling to assess data quality, structure, and anomalies, and recommend remediation strategies.
Support the definition of data standards, naming conventions, and reference data.


Azure & Databricks Development Support


Collaborate with engineering teams to design data solutions using Azure services (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
Work closely with Databricks developers to ensure data transformations align with approved data models.
Review and validate data pipelines to ensure compliance with business rules and architectural guidelines.


Business Analysis & Banking Domain Engagement


Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data and reporting requirements.
Apply strong understanding of personal banking products (such as accounts, customers, transactions, lending, and payments) to data design decisions.
Translate business processes and regulatory requirements into structured data representations.


SQL & Data Validation


Write advanced SQL queries for data exploration, validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation.
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and data validation activities by comparing source and target datasets.
Identify data quality issues and work with technical teams to resolve root causes.


Agile Delivery & Collaboration


Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, grooming, stand ups, reviews).
Estimate and plan data design and analysis tasks in coordination with scrum teams.
Maintain accurate documentation and user stories using Jira and Confluence.
Support continuous improvement of agile data delivery practices.


Communication & Stakeholder Management


Communicate complex data concepts clearly to technical and non technical stakeholders.
Lead data design reviews, walkthroughs, and stakeholder discussions.
Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data analysts or designers when required.


Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Core Technical Skills


Strong experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure Data Lake, Azure SQL, Synapse, or related services).
Hands on experience with Databricks for data transformations and analytics.
Advanced SQL skills for data analysis, profiling, and validation.
Proven expertise in Data Profiling, Data Mapping, and Data Modeling (logical and physical).


Business & Domain Skills


Strong Business Analysis skills, including requirements elicitation and documentation.
Solid knowledge of personal banking products and data domains.
Ability to translate business needs into scalable and reusable data designs.


Tools & Ways of Working


Experience using Jira for backlog and sprint management.
Proficient in Confluence for documentation and knowledge sharing.
Demonstrated experience working in Agile/Scrum delivery environments.


Soft Skills


Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration abilities.
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to team goals.


________________________________________

Nice to Have (Optional)


Experience in regulated financial services environments.
Exposure to data governance, metadata management, or data lineage tools.
Familiarity with reporting or downstream analytics use cases.


"CGI is providing a reasonable estimate of the pay range for this role. The determination of this range includes factors such as skill set level, geographic market, experience and training, and licenses and certifications. Compensation decisions depend on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $80,000–$130,000. This role is an existing vacancy."

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

Title: Data Governance Analyst
Description:
Data Governance Analyst




Who We Are




Teranet is Canada’s leader in the delivery and transformation of statutory registry services with extensive expertise in land and commercial registries. We also market insightful property and data solutions, as well as practice management automation to thousands of customers in real estate, financial services, government, utilities, and legal markets.




Connect. Grow. Thrive Together.




To learn more about who we are visit our website: www.teranet.ca




About the Role




The Data Governance Analyst plays a key role in supporting the organization’s Data and AI Governance and Management Program to ensure enterprise data is accurate, secure, and effectively managed throughout its lifecycle. This position works collaboratively with data owners, stewards, and technical teams to implement governance policies, maintain metadata and data catalogs, and support compliance with privacy and information security requirements. The role provides essential expertise and operational capacity as the organization continues to expand its data lake environment and integrate diverse data types, including unstructured data sources and supporting the enablement of Governed AI.




What You’ll Be Doing




Data Governance Operations

Support the ongoing operation of the Data Governance Program in alignment with enterprise frameworks and standards.
Maintain governance documentation, including policies, procedures, and data stewardship guidelines.
Assist in the prioritization and tracking of governance initiatives, issues, and metrics.




Metadata and Catalog Management

Maintain and enhance metadata repositories, business glossaries, and data catalogs to improve data discovery and understanding.
Partner with technical teams to ensure data lineage, classification, and tagging are accurate and up to date.
Support integration of metadata tools with other data management platforms.




Data Quality and Compliance

Develop and monitor data quality metrics, identify data quality issues, and support remediation with business and technical stakeholders.
Collaborate with Information Security, Compliance, and Privacy teams to ensure adherence to regulatory and internal data management standards.
Assist in audits, risk assessments, and governance reviews as required.




Stakeholder Engagement and Stewardship

Partner with data owners, stewards, and custodians across business units to define data standards and accountability.
Support training and communication efforts to build awareness of data governance principles and responsibilities.
Facilitate cross-functional meetings and working groups to address governance priorities.




Process Improvement and Enablement

Identify opportunities to automate or streamline governance processes and data quality monitoring.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of governance practices, tools, and reporting.
Support maturity assessments and roadmap planning for future governance enhancements.




About You




What will you need to be successful?




You are someone who:

Is keen to understand the business – data analytics cannot be done in a vacuum – we need to be close to the business so that we understand what we are trying to solve for and what the data means
Brings a proportional risk mindset – to analytics by identifying data quality, governance, privacy, and downstream business risks, ensuring insights are reliable, responsible, and decision-ready.
Tells stories from data – data analytics is not data dump – it needs to be meaningful and intuitive to the end user in order to realize the inherent business value
Challenges the status quo – the state of business and the field of data analytics are constantly evolving, and we need to as well.Assumptions and old ways of doing things are open season, as long as you bring along workable and relevant solutions articulated in a professional manner. Proactively pursue and learn about new data sets, analytical tools and methodologies to solve problems in new and innovative ways.
Is good at problem solving – someone who can “connect the dots”, and have a logical and methodical way to tackle new and complex problems
Takes pride in the work – your work is diligent and thorough, and you are proud of your reputation for high-quality and reliable work
Is a quick study – the successful candidate must be curious, resourceful, and dig into the weeds to ask the right questions and figure things out independently
Is a good communicator – someone who is polished and can communicate effectively with internal and external parties to ensure alignment on data analysis and reporting needs




Technical qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in quantitative field: Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering
Minimum 2 years of work experience as a Data Analyst
Proficiency in SQL, Python, Spark, Excel, and/or Power BI/Tableau
Experience with a Data Governance Platform such Atacama, Collibra, Informatica, Purview etc. or would be an asset
Familiarity with Audit, Risk, or Compliance would be an asset




Let’s Talk Pay




We believe in being upfront about pay and helping you make informed decisions about your career. The annual pay range for this role is $72,000 - $88,000 CAD, inclusive of base salary and target incentive pay. We understand that great talent comes in many forms, each with unique skills, experience, and potential. Your salary will be tailored to reflect the experience you bring and the impact you’re ready to have on this role.




At Teranet, we also know that compensation extends far beyond a pay cheque. Along with your cash compensation, we offer a comprehensive package which includes the following:




100% Employer Paid Health Benefit Plan
Employer Matching Retirement Savings Plan
Paid Vacation, Floater Days & Sick Leaves
Maternity, Parental and/or Adoption Leave Top-Up Programs
Corporate Discounts & GoodLife Group Rate Membership
Employee Assistance Program – for you and your loved ones!




Our Human Approach to Hiring




At Teranet, we use smart technology to make hiring faster and more efficient. Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools help us review applications and identify strong matches—but the real decisions? Those are made by our awesome people.




Every interaction you’ll have, and every decision we make, is led by humans. AI may support us, but it’s human connection that drives every hire.




At Teranet, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we live and serve. If you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.




Come As You Are. We Like You That Way!

What You’ll Be Doing


Data Governance Operations

  • Support the ongoing operation of the Data Governance Program in alignment with enterprise frameworks and standards.
  • Maintain governance documentation, including policies, procedures, and data stewardship guidelines.
  • Assist in the prioritization and tracking of governance initiatives, issues, and metrics.


Metadata and Catalog Management

  • Maintain and enhance metadata repositories, business glossaries, and data catalogs to improve data discovery and understanding.
  • Partner with technical teams to ensure data lineage, classification, and tagging are accurate and up to date.
  • Support integration of metadata tools with other data management platforms.


Data Quality and Compliance

  • Develop and monitor data quality metrics, identify data quality issues, and support remediation with business and technical stakeholders.
  • Collaborate with Information Security, Compliance, and Privacy teams to ensure adherence to regulatory and internal data management standards.
  • Assist in audits, risk assessments, and governance reviews as required.


Stakeholder Engagement and Stewardship

  • Partner with data owners, stewards, and custodians across business units to define data standards and accountability.
  • Support training and communication efforts to build awareness of data governance principles and responsibilities.
  • Facilitate cross-functional meetings and working groups to address governance priorities.


Process Improvement and Enablement

  • Identify opportunities to automate or streamline governance processes and data quality monitoring.
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of governance practices, tools, and reporting.
  • Support maturity assessments and roadmap planning for future governance enhancements.


About You


What will you need to be successful?


You are someone who:

  • Is keen to understand the business – data analytics cannot be done in a vacuum – we need to be close to the business so that we understand what we are trying to solve for and what the data means
  • Brings a proportional risk mindset – to analytics by identifying data quality, governance, privacy, and downstream business risks, ensuring insights are reliable, responsible, and decision-ready.
  • Tells stories from data – data analytics is not data dump – it needs to be meaningful and intuitive to the end user in order to realize the inherent business value
  • Challenges the status quo – the state of business and the field of data analytics are constantly evolving, and we need to as well.Assumptions and old ways of doing things are open season, as long as you bring along workable and relevant solutions articulated in a professional manner. Proactively pursue and learn about new data sets, analytical tools and methodologies to solve problems in new and innovative ways.
  • Is good at problem solving – someone who can “connect the dots”, and have a logical and methodical way to tackle new and complex problems
  • Takes pride in the work – your work is diligent and thorough, and you are proud of your reputation for high-quality and reliable work
  • Is a quick study – the successful candidate must be curious, resourceful, and dig into the weeds to ask the right questions and figure things out independently
  • Is a good communicator – someone who is polished and can communicate effectively with internal and external parties to ensure alignment on data analysis and reporting needs


Technical qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in quantitative field: Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering
  • Minimum 2 years of work experience as a Data Analyst
  • Proficiency in SQL, Python, Spark, Excel, and/or Power BI/Tableau
  • Experience with a Data Governance Platform such Atacama, Collibra, Informatica, Purview etc. or would be an asset
  • Familiarity with Audit, Risk, or Compliance would be an asset


Let’s Talk Pay


We believe in being upfront about pay and helping you make informed decisions about your career. The annual pay range for this role is $72,000 - $88,000 CAD, inclusive of base salary and target incentive pay. We understand that great talent comes in many forms, each with unique skills, experience, and potential. Your salary will be tailored to reflect the experience you bring and the impact you’re ready to have on this role.


At Teranet, we also know that compensation extends far beyond a pay cheque. Along with your cash compensation, we offer a comprehensive package which includes the following:


  • 100% Employer Paid Health Benefit Plan
  • Employer Matching Retirement Savings Plan
  • Paid Vacation, Floater Days & Sick Leaves
  • Maternity, Parental and/or Adoption Leave Top-Up Programs
  • Corporate Discounts & GoodLife Group Rate Membership
  • Employee Assistance Program – for you and your loved ones!


Our Human Approach to Hiring


At Teranet, we use smart technology to make hiring faster and more efficient. Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools help us review applications and identify strong matches—but the real decisions? Those are made by our awesome people.


Every interaction you’ll have, and every decision we make, is led by humans. AI may support us, but it’s human connection that drives every hire.


At Teranet, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we live and serve. If you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.


Come As You Are. We Like You That Way!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LeverageTek is actively seeking a Data Analyst for a full-time, 14-month term opportunity with its Ottawa-based customer.







Work Location

Ottawa, ON (Hybrid/1 day per week onsite)







Key Tasks

Gather, clean, and analyze complaints and business data to support core performance metrics
Develop actionable insights to improve decision-making and operational efficiency
Extract and manipulate data using SQL and other data tools
Design, build, and maintain dashboards using Tableau or Power BI
Create and deliver reports using enterprise tools such as Microsoft SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS)
Conduct data mining, statistical analysis, and trend identification
Support preparation of mid-year and annual reports
Automate reporting processes to enhance efficiency and accuracy
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical solutions
Present analytical findings in a clear, concise manner to support business strategy
Contribute to special projects as assigned







Key Qualifications

Minimum 2 years of hands-on experience with data tools and analytics (SQL + Power BI + SSRS + Advanced Excel): Ability to extract, analyze, and visualize data using SQL and BI tools is core to the role.
Minimum 2 years of experience Data Analytics: Analyzing data to generate insight
Experience with story telling with data: Communicating insights to the Business/Executives







Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field
Strong experience working with SQL and data querying
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI
Experience with statistical analysis and data interpretation
Strong understanding of databases and data warehouse concepts
Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to present complex data in a clear and concise manner
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines
Highly organized with strong attention to detail and accuracy
Proven ability to work independently and take initiative
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, with advanced Excel skills
Experience with enterprise reporting tools (e.g., SSRS)







Assets

Bilingualism (English/French) is an asset
Experience in telecommunications or television industry is an asset
Familiarity with programming languages such as Python or R is an asset







Salary Range: $80,000 - $85,000

This is an existing position vacancy.










About LeverageTek Staffing Solutions

Founded in 2003, LeverageTek provides end-to-end, cross-functional staffing solutions throughout North America. We are a trusted partner to leading private and public sector organizations and experts in talent solutions that create optimal business outcomes.

Don’t let our name fool you. Our roots are in technology, but we are also a proven leader in accounting and finance, sales and marketing, human resources, supply chain, and legal talent acquisition. We offer contract and permanent staffing, executive search, talent mapping, management consultancy, and contractor payroll management.

LeverageTek is an equal opportunity employer. We offer a welcoming and inclusive environment in service to one another, our customers, the candidates we represent, and the diverse communities we call home. We do all of this with kindness, empathy, and respect for each other. LeverageTek is committed to employment equity and creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, and marital status.

Accessibility accommodations are available upon request

Title: TEDRA Data Analyst
Description:
Ready to build the future with AI?

At Genpact, we don’t just keep up with technology—we set the pace. AI and digital innovation are redefining industries, and we’re leading the charge. Genpact’s AI Gigafactory, our industry-first accelerator, is an example of how we’re scaling advanced technology solutions to help global enterprises work smarter, grow faster, and transform at scale. From large-scale models to agentic AI, our breakthrough solutions tackle companies’ most complex challenges.




If you thrive in a fast-moving, innovation-driven environment, love building and deploying cutting-edge AI solutions, and want to push the boundaries of what’s possible, this is your moment.




Genpact (NYSE: G) is an advanced technology services and solutions company that delivers lasting value for leading enterprises globally. Through our deep business knowledge, operational excellence, and cutting-edge solutions – we help companies across industries get ahead and stay ahead. Powered by curiosity, courage, and innovation, our teams implement data, technology, and AI to create tomorrow, today. Get to know us at genpact.com and on LinkedIn, X, YouTube, and Facebook.




Inviting applications for the role of Lead Consultant, Data Analyst

In this role, you will be responsible for coding, testing and delivering high quality deliverables, and should be willing to learn new technologies.




Responsibilities

Collaborate with cross divisional project managers, technology product owners, and firm data office engineering teams to ensure alignment on goals and deliverables for the global regulatory program delivering data quality reporting on critical reporting
Conducts a communication style that effectively “bridges the gaps” between policy, central tooling, program governance, and delivery.
Work directly with development teams to educate them on intended outcomes and available data governance tools, data quality integration and JIRA familiarity on reporting, managing and issue tracking.
Troubleshoot roadblocks encountered by development teams.
Understand and familiarize Data Architecture & Management principles and well as the requirements of the BCBS 239 framework and will understand and familiarize with Firms’ Data Policy and responsibilities and commitments within the Application Development organization.
Identify and mitigate risks, manage dependencies, and resolve issues proactively using the Agile framework
Partner with Firmwide Data Office to enhance strategic tooling for Cataloging & Lineage.
Willingness to work beyond these responsibilities for general assistance to the program.




Qualifications we seek in you!

Minimum Qualifications

BE/B Tech/MCA
Excellent written and verbal communication skills




Preferred Qualifications/ Skills

Experience as a Business or Data Analyst with strong communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively and propose ideas and concepts.
Familiarity with data governance concepts of cataloging, ownership, lineage, and data quality
Experience working with global teams in multiple locations ideally in the finance industry
Express and influence complex ideas and deliverables in a simple way with clear benefits
Is a structured, highly organized individual with the ability to self-manage, work with a level of ambiguity and has a pro-active approach
Focus on fostering an inclusive environment that welcomes diverse perspectives
Ability to understand technical solutions and apply them in similar situations; though not a coding position, the candidate needs to be able to work at an API and configuration level




Why join Genpact?

• Lead AI-first transformation – Build and scale AI solutions that redefine industries

• Make an impact – Drive change for global enterprises and solve business challenges that matter

• Accelerate your career—Gain hands-on experience, world-class training, mentorship, and AI certifications to advance your skills

• Grow with the best – Learn from top engineers, data scientists, and AI experts in a dynamic, fast-moving workplace

• Committed to ethical AI – Work in an environment where governance, transparency, and security are at the core of everything we build

• Thrive in a values-driven culture – Our courage, curiosity, and incisiveness - built on a foundation of integrity and inclusion - allow your ideas to fuel progress

Come join the 140,000+ coders, tech shapers, and growth makers at Genpact and take your career in the only direction that matters: Up.

Let’s build tomorrow together.




Genpact is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for all positions without regard to race, color, religion or belief, sex, age, national origin, citizenship status, marital status, military/veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, physical or mental disability or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws. Genpact is committed to creating a dynamic work environment that values respect and integrity, customer focus, and innovation.

Furthermore, please do note that Genpact does not charge fees to process job applications and applicants are not required to pay to participate in our hiring process in any other way. Examples of such scams include purchasing a 'starter kit,' paying to apply, or purchasing equipment or training.

Title: Fresher Data Analyst
Description:
About Joveo:

Every company says they're "AI-first." We actually are. Joveo's recruitment

advertising platform processes millions of hiring decisions through machine

learning, real-time bidding, and predictive analytics — helping the world's

largest employers find the right people, faster and fairer. But we're not

done. Not even close.




Role: Fresher Data Analyst

Location: Remote




Role Overview:

We are hiring a Fresher Data Analyst to start your analytics career

working with Joveo's real production data. You will learn SQL,

dashboarding, and data storytelling by analyzing actual business

questions — and your insights will directly influence decisions

made by teams across the company.




Key Responsibilities:

- Write SQL queries to pull, transform, and analyze data from production systems

- Build dashboards and reports for product, operations, and business stakeholders

- Investigate business questions and translate findings into clear recommendations

- Learn data visualization tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, or Metabase)

- Support senior analysts and data scientists on larger analytical projects

- Grow your analytical, statistical, and data storytelling skills over time




Required Skills & Qualifications:

- Recent graduate or up to 1 year of analytics or data-adjacent experience

- Bachelor's degree in any field with strong analytical or quantitative focus

- Working knowledge of SQL and basic data manipulation

- Familiarity with Excel, Google Sheets, and spreadsheet data work

- Exposure to at least one BI or visualization tool is a plus

- Curiosity about data, patterns, and translating numbers into stories




Equal Opportunity Employer:

Joveo is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building an

inclusive workplace and welcome applications from all qualified individuals

regardless of race, color, ethnicity, nationality, gender, gender identity

or expression, sexual orientation, age, religion, disability, marital status,

or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. All hiring decisions

are made solely on the basis of qualifications, skills, and demonstrated

ability.
